name: Cueva del Gato Trail
name_original: Monumento Natural Cueva del Gato – running por el Río Guadiaro
description: Jog the moderate 3.2-mile Cueva del Gato Trail through Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park, featuring the Cat Cave and Charco Frío.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Benaoján traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park, characterized by dramatic limestone formations and the Guadiaro River valley. The region offers varied terrain, from riverside paths to mountainous inclines and descents, suitable for different running preferences. Lush vegetation, including holm oaks and cork oaks, lines many trails, providing a scenic backdrop for running. This area's natural features create a dynamic environment for outdoor activity. Best jogging routes around Benaoján The most popular jogging route is Cueva del Gato Trail, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail that takes 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows an accessible path along the Guadiaro River towards the Cueva del Gato. Another top favourite among local runners is Charco de la Barranca – Hermitage Pond loop from Jimera de Líbar, a difficult 26.9 miles (43.4 km) path. This extensive loop features significant elevation changes through varied natural landscapes. Local runners also love the Arab Bridge of Ronda – Cat Cave loop from Benaojan-Montejaque, a 35.0 miles (56.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, olive groves, and vineyards, often completed in about 6 hours 37 minutes. Jogging around Benaoján is defined by dramatic limestone landscapes, the Guadiaro River valley, and varied inclines and descents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain trails. The routes in Benaoján are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 2,000 runners have used komoot to explore Benaoján's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Benaoján?
A: There are over 120 running routes available around Benaoján, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels. The komoot community has explored these trails over 2,000 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Benaoján?
A: Jogging routes in Benaoján traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park. You can expect varied terrain, from accessible riverside paths along the Guadiaro River to mountainous inclines and descents through dramatic limestone formations. Many trails are lined with lush vegetation, including holm oaks and cork oaks.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or families in Benaoján?
A: Yes, Benaoján offers several easy running routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996238607/cueva-del-gato-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Gato Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.1 km route that follows an accessible path along the Guadiaro River, making it suitable for a less strenuous run.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Benaoján area?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Barranca – Hermitage Pond loop from Benaojan-Montejaque&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging 17.7 km circular path. The region research also mentions various circular routes connecting Ronda and Benaoján, some stretching up to 20 km.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can see while jogging?
A: While jogging in Benaoján, you can encounter several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/857563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cat Cave&lt;/a&gt; (Cueva del Gato) is a significant natural monument, and many routes lead towards it, offering views of its crystal-clear waters. The Guadiaro River valley itself is picturesque, and the area is known for its karst formations and other caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Hundidero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Benaoján?
A: Benaoján is located within the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park. While many trails are generally accessible, it's important to check specific park regulations regarding dogs, especially concerning protected areas or wildlife. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Benaoján?
A: The Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park is known for its significant rainfall, contributing to lush vegetation. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for running. Summers can be hot, while winters might bring cooler, wetter conditions, which can be refreshing but require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in the Benaoján area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer runs, a section of the GR 7, an extensive long-distance hiking trail spanning Spain, passes through the Benaoján area. This provides excellent opportunities for extended jogging amidst stunning scenery. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31739695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arab Bridge of Ronda – Cat Cave loop from Benaojan-Montejaque&lt;/a&gt; are quite long, at 56.3 km.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Benaoján?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Runners often praise the dramatic limestone landscapes, the serene Guadiaro River valley, and the varied inclines and descents that provide a dynamic environment for outdoor activity.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in Benaoján?
A: Benaoján has a train station, Benaoján-Montejaque, which can serve as a starting point for several routes. This makes it possible to access some trailheads via public transport, connecting the village to other towns like Ronda and Jimera de Líbar.

Q: Where can I find parking when accessing running routes in Benaoján?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Benaoján village and Benaoján-Montejaque train station. Many trailheads are accessible from these points. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations or designated areas, especially during peak seasons.

Q: Are there any routes that offer cultural or historical points of interest?
A: Yes, some routes offer cultural insights. For example, the region research mentions a 7 km walk from Benaoján station to the Cueva de la Pileta, renowned for its prehistoric rock art, offering a distinct cultural and natural experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/805294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mozarabic Rock-Hewn Hermitage of Virgen de la Cabeza&lt;/a&gt; is another historical highlight in the area.
tips: This wonderful place has been declared a Natural Monument and with good reason. It is a cave with a natural waterfall that marks the outlet of the Guadiaro river after its channel hides inside the rock, traveling through caves and underground galleries for almost 4 km from the site known as Hundidero del Gato upstream. Here you will also find a picnic area and benches that make it the ideal place for a break and appreciate this wonderful environment. Take care and respect the indications, it is forbidden to enter the cave.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: The immediate vicinity of the Cat Cave offers relatively easy and short paths, suitable for casual jogging. There's also an easy 6 km route from Benaoján to the cave with minimal gradient, often following wide tracks and quiet lanes. However, some longer routes in the broader area can involve narrow, eroded sections and short, steep ascents and descents, making continuous jogging challenging. Be aware that trails can be muddy in colder months.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the Cueva del Gato Trail?
faqs.answer: There is a car park available in the immediate vicinity of the Cat Cave. From there, it's a pleasant and easy stroll of 150 to 500 meters to the cave entrance and its natural pool.

faqs.question: Is the Cueva del Gato Trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: Yes, for beginners, the shorter, flatter sections around the Cat Cave and the 6 km route from Benaoján are ideal. These paths have minimal gradient and a good surface for jogging. More challenging routes exist in the wider area for experienced trail runners.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Joggers can enjoy the stunning scenery of the Guadiaro River Valley, the dramatic limestone mountains of Benaoján, and beautiful countryside. The main highlight is the Cat Cave itself, with its unique cat-face-shaped entrance, and the breathtaking Charco Frío, a natural pool with crystal-clear, very cold water.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Guadiaro River Valley is a rich habitat for various waterbirds and riverside species, including herons, cormorants, and nightingales. If you're lucky, you might even spot Iberian Ibex on the rocks above the Cat Cave.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable in many seasons. However, the Charco Frío natural pool is particularly refreshing during warmer months. Be aware that trails can become muddy in colder months, which might affect jogging conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Cueva del Gato Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park. While dogs are generally permitted on trails within the park, it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially near the river and cave areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Access to the Cueva del Gato Trail and the immediate area around the Cat Cave typically does not require a permit or entrance fee. However, for certain restricted access areas or specific activities within the broader Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park, permits might be necessary. It's advisable to check with local park authorities for any specific regulations if you plan to explore beyond the main trail.

faqs.question: Are there any unique natural features or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The trail's namesake, the Cat Cave, is a striking natural monument. In front of it lies the Charco Frío, a stunning natural pool fed by a waterfall. The area is also part of the extensive Hundidero-Gato Cave System, an important speleological site, though public entry into the cave itself is prohibited for safety reasons.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Ronda - Benaoján, Via Serrana, Estación de Benaojan - Jimera de Líbar, Río Guadiaro, Sendero del Río Guadiaro, and Ronda - Estación de Benaojan trails.

faqs.question: Can I swim in the natural pool at Cueva del Gato?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Charco Frío, the natural pool in front of the Cat Cave, is a popular spot for a refreshing dip. Its water is crystal-clear and very cold, making it particularly inviting during warmer months.

faqs.question: What makes this trail a Natural Monument?
faqs.answer: The Cueva del Gato area has been declared a Natural Monument of Andalusia due to its unique beauty and geological significance. It's part of the Hundidero-Gato Cave System, one of Andalusia's most important cave systems, and also holds Palaeolithic and post-Palaeolithic rock art, adding to its cultural and natural value.
